entry.title: Re: move & move back of table changes order of objects =>...
entry.content:
Panels are your friend here 🙂

The above example I have 2 Tabs:  Tab_1 & Tab_2
pnl_1 sits on Tab_1  
pnl_2 sits on Tab_2

Table_1 sits inside pnl_1 
The listbox sits outside the panel - ergo it's always on top.



Code used:

The button on Tab_1 used to move the Table contains this code:
pnl_2.moveWidget(Table_1);
TabStrip_1.setSelectedKey('Tab_2');
Table_1.openNavigationPanel();

The button on Tab_2 uses this code:

pnl_1.moveWidget(Table_1);
TabStrip_1.setSelectedKey('Tab_1');

entry.title: How to handle Invoice by Delivery Document with Down payment request
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I would like to know how to handle Invoice by Delivery Document with Down payment request

My client's business normal business flow to invoice by delivery documents. Over 80% order needs to do so since all business are export orders to oversea. Some orders they needs to get down payment and they would like their sales team to prepare down payment request to smooth the flow to book down payment in accounting team.

I check that there is a flow in BKJ for sales order processing with customer down payment request. It seems goods in Down payment request part.

https://education.hana.ondemand.com/education/pub/s4/index.html?library=library.txt&show=book!BO_343579C1CFDE56B0#book!BO_143A8BD19EDC0F8E

However, this is not working with my client business since BKJ cannot create billing document form delivery document. It is becase CBAO setup in S4H public cloud is fixed with order-billing relevant instead of delivery billing relevent.

Also, when using CBAO, the sales order cannot combine other delivery document to create billing documents together. The billing document must be combined for the sales orders with delivery in my client business to fulfill the custom requirement as well as end-customer requirement.

Are there any configuration I could do to allow Order with billing plan to create billing document from delivery document in S4H public cloud/

Or Do there any other flows I could follow in order to achieve Invoice by Delivery Document with Down payment request?

Please advise.

entry.title: Solution for proof batch delivery for outbound delivery
entry.content:
Dear Sir

Case:


The customer requests that the expense and Revenue must be in the same month according to the standard work cycle of the sap that is being worked on now, a sales order is created for a quantity of 100 pieces, and then an invoice is created for 100 pieces, then delivery is created for 100 pieces, and then PGI OBD for 100 pieces, but the Stock are still already in the Storage location and 100 pieces are sent in separate batches according to the needs of the Sold-to-party request to different Ship-to-Party forwarders.


business needs


We need a solution that confirms and tracks and prints the delivery of batches to different Ship-to-Party forwarders and each batch has a different freight order according to the location of the Ship-to-Party to which it will be shipped.
And the ability to track the quantities that have been delivered and the remaining quantities that have not been delivered for the same delivery.

Thanks

entry.title: Tips to reference a SAC add in Excel Table as any "standard" Excel Table
entry.content:
Recently we’ve introduced the capability to change the name of the add-in table to make it more business oriented. Then, from a new menu it's possible to rename each table. Once renamed the new name can be retrieved via the "SAP.GETTABLE NAME" and visible in the name range area.

We get the question how to make standard Excel array/table formulas work on SACTable1 object (or renamed object, "Budget" in our example)  just like normal Excel tables to be used on standard Excel functions.

Let's take Excel   "FILTER" function as example. This function lets you filter a range of data based on criteria you define based on multiple conditions and display only the columns you need—all automatically.  This "range " in our case, is retrieved by the Add in table name, which refers to a dynamic table range as any Excel table.   

The specificity here is that the headers need to be removed from the selection of the Add in table. And the filter function will be  =FILTER(OFFSET(SACBudget;4;);INDEX(OFFSET(SACBudget;4;;);0;3)="Racing"). 

Except that the add in table object works as any standard Excel table and can be easily used in dynamic array/formulas. 

Try it by yourself with your dynamic formulas!

SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Office

SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Office 

SAP Analytics Cloud for planning

entry.title: S/4 HANA Cloudification Guide for Developer: Navigating to Released APIs from Classic Objects
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog aims to provide a simple guide for discovering newly released APIs that replace traditional SAP objects, including tables, function modules, class objects, and more.

Whether your organization is implementing Clean Core mindset in S4/HANA On-remise or going through cloud migration(SAP RISE Private Cloud/Public Cloud), one of the first steps towards cloudification is to assess the existing custom solutions are clean core compatible. 

The 3-tier extensibility model is a classification that provides rough idea of how much risk of system upgrade will disrupt your custom solution and how protected and stable it is. In the prerequisite of Tier 1, which by definition has the smallest risk of getting affected by upgrade disruption, states the enforced usage of released APIs. Using released objects is a must and a first step towards clean core way of building custom solutions in your SAP system. 

The challenge with deprecated objects

Before this clean core concept or 3-tier extension model were introduced, the standard way to extend SAP ERP core is using now so-called classic extensibility, which corresponds to the 3rd-tier in the 3tier-extensibility model. Organizations operating with SAP before this new concept are most likely full of classic extensibility solution. If their system is rated with today's clean core standard, the result will probably say it's not cloud ready/compatible. Number of SAP objects used in the classic solution are now labeled as deprecated in the new standard, resulting in renovating all the custom solution that uses the deprecated objects. To adapt to the new standard, it is crucial to map the deprecated objects to the new released APIs to ensure the functionality and the performance of the custom solutions.

(This is, of course, not customer's fault as back then the solutions were probably implemented with the newest technology, insight and standard of that time.)

With this in mind, let's look at what's the easiest way to find released APIs for each classic ABAP objects.

1. Classic objects ➡️ Released objects   

Database Tables

The easiest way is to use Fiori App: View Browser(F2170). Check out ➡️ Finding released CDS views for classic SAP tables  for detailed steps to find released CDS views for classic SAP tables. 

The second easiest way is to use SAP Cloudification Repository, which is also covered in the blog above (however not all objects are listed).

Function Modules / Class

Open the function module in question with ABAP Development Tool(ADT). Open Properties->API State and the successor object is specified.

Example below checks for function module BAPI_PO_CHANGE and class object CL_SD_DOCUMENT_FLOW_RT.

 

 

Alternatively, you can use SAP Cloudification Repository(however not all objects are listed).

 

Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) Definitions 

Open the BAdI Definition in question with ABAP Development Tool(ADT). When opening the object, the enhancement spot that uses the BAdI Definition should open and all the BAdI Definitions will be listed. Right click on the BAdI Definition in question -> API State -> Display Use System-Internally.

Example below checks for BAdI Definition MEOUT_BAPI_CUST.

Release state is shown as well as the successor objects if you click Next.

 

 

 

Function Interfaces, Authorization Object, Data Types..etc.

Successor for rest of objects can be found in ADT same way as function module and class objects.   

 

2. Exploring released APIs

In ABAP Development Tool(ADT)

ADT offers tree views to list all the objects depending on the object release status. You can also customize the tree as you see fit by adding Application Component, Packages and Software Component as an extra layer. It does not only show the released objects but also deprecated objects as well as deprecated objects with successors. 

Check out ➡️ Exploring released APIs in ABAP Development Tool for step-by-step guide. 

 

 

Trees that show Released Objects, Deprecated Objects and Deprecated Objects with Successors

 

In SAP Accelerator Hub

SAP Accelerator Hub is an alternative to find API's for those that don't have developer access and want to explore APIs from business context. It contains not just APIs for tier1/tier2 cloud development, but also out-of-box template for business processes, integration scenarios, events based solutions and everything you need for extension your SAP ERP system. Choose your product(S/4 Public/Private Edition, BTP, etc) and start exploring.

Filter the package and explore released APIs with your desired business area.  

 

Appendix

Released APIs

APIs marked as released are indication that they are technically stable and is the only option available in tier 1 extension to communicate with SAP backend. SAP categorizes the released objects in 5 ways depending on the types and the intension of the extension. In this blog, we focus on the cloudification of SAP objects used for custom development hence will only touch on C1 Contract (Use System-Internally).

  • Extend (C0)
  • Use System-Internally (C1)
  • Use as Remote API (C2)
  • Manage Configuration Content (C3)
  • Use in ABAP-Managed Database Procedures (C4)

C1 Contract (Use System-Internally)

Released in C1 contracts indicate that the object is stable enough to be used in Key-User Extension(Custom fields and Custom Logic apps) and Developer Extensibility(custom solution developed solely by ABAP Development for Cloud language scope). The release state is managed separately for each usage and can be found in ABAP Development Tool.

entry.title: Re: BAS Remote Extension Host Terminated
entry.content:
The SAP support developers helped us investigate this issue. Even though we could not find which Extension was causing this problem, but whatever extension it was, it kept re-activating itself and as it did that it made the Storyboard popup generate again every minute or so.

So as a workaround we created a brand new DevSpace, without any additional extensions, downloaded the projects from old DevSpace and moved into the new DevSpace. And the issue is gone.

It's unfortunate that we couldn't find the culprit extension, as it makes it difficult now to trust any of the extensions available for BAS. But at least the problem is gone and we can continue developing.

Many thanks to SAP support developers.

entry.title: Where can I start learning consumption-based planning in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Using Consumption-Based Planning in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This course provides a comprehensive understanding of consumption-based planning in SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, consultants and business users will be able to:

  • Describe the features and methods of consumption-based planning
  • Explain and control the steps of a planning run specifically for consumption-based planning
  • Perform a planning run and analyze the results
  • Decide on the usage and definition of MRP areas
  • Run a forecast and use the forecast results for planning

Prerequisites
For learners who need to learn the basic procurement business scenarios, please visit the learning journey: 
Exploring the Basics of External Procurement in SAP S/4HANA

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务
entry.content:
Hi ，

我们遇到的业务场景是这样的：

A公司和B公司是集团内部的两个公司，A公司是销售公司，B公司是制造公司。

1）A公司接收到外部客户的订单需求后，在A公司创建对外部客户的销售订单；

2）A公司向B公司采购，B公司基于A公司的订单需求进行生产。需要在B公司实现按销售订单（或按项目需求）进行生产追踪的要求。

我尝试想用5D2-高级公司销售，但是，在论坛中查到5D2是不支持以下业务场景的，

  • Make-to-order
  • 与WBS集成

相关贴子：高级公司间销售限制；

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/%E9%AB%98%E7%BA%A7%E5%85%AC%E5%8F%B8%E9%97%B4%E9%94%80%E5%94%AE%E9%99%90%E5%88%B6-%E5%85%AC%E6%9C%89%E4%BA%91erp2302%E7%89%88%E6%9C%AC/ba-p/13559888

我也尝试想用BDN+1P9，但是发现1P9，在B公司是没有销售订单的，无法实现上述需求。

我的疑问是：

在集团内部公司间关联交易的情况下，需要在B公司，即制造公司实现按销售订单生产或按项目生产的模型，可以使用什么Scope Item？

entry.title: Re: IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Producti...
entry.content:
hi Parthi4,

"What issues/problems we get if we do change/update the Formatting of Cells in IBP Formatting Sheet directly in IBP Production system ? "

-> technically, i don't see a risk, as the Formatting Sheets & Templates are just a way to display the information in Excel.

I think it depends on:

- how many users are already using the template that you want to change/update. If the template is going to be fully changed, it's best to communicate to any users first

- are there any jobs ran directly from the templates (eg statistical forecast runs)? If yes , then the updated template will probably need to follow the same applied filters as the "old" one.

 

But have you considered first to copy the old template , save it as Favorite in Prod -> change it/update -> test it?

A favorite template is not shared by default with anyone, so any changes you made in Prod will not affect anyone.

Datapshere try to run the loading but the same message appears :"The source connector has accepted the initial load partitioning request. The next attempt will be after ..."

Do you know why init replication flow still retrying to load data ? (is not a delta)

Do you already have the same issue ? 

Thanks for the help,

Best regards

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: We need a solution for product Catalog attributes
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Data migration sequence for Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Dear Elena, 
from a technical view the sequence in the UI is somewhat the same, because when you create rate plan in the product app it is also created when you close the detail screen of the rate plan. 
When using the APIs, the sequence you're mentioning below is the best one to use:
1. Create rate plan templates
2. Create rate plan with reference to previously created rate plan template ID
3. Create Product and assign previously created rate plan ID 

You could also create first the product, followed by rate plan templates and then rate plans, but that would require that you update the product again with the rate plans - which is more complicated.

Best
Carsten

entry.title: STIHL optimized Sales and Production Planning with SAP Solutions for Synchronized Planning
entry.content:
Grown from a one-man business into a global leader STIHL Group develops, manufactures and distributes power tools for professional forestry and agriculture as well as for garden and landscape maintenance, the construction sector and private garden owners. The product range is complemented by digital solutions and services. Products are distributed through authorized dealers and STIHL’s own online-shops, which will be expanded internationally over the next few years – including 44 sales and marketing subsidiaries, about 120 importers and more than 55,000 servicing dealers in over 160 countries.

Founded in 1926 and with headquarters in Southern Germany, STIHL has been the world's top-selling chainsaw brand since 1971. With a workforce of almost 20,000 employees, the family-owned business generated 5.3 billion euros in revenue in 2023.

Siloed Supply Chain Planning Systems insufficient for Informed Decision-Making
In the past, STIHL’s subsidiaries and production companies relied on their own SAP EEC system, deployed on premise, with a heterogenous landscape for supply chain planning processes 35 sales and 8 production companies  that were tailored to the needs of each subsidiary. “We lacked an end-to-end consolidated view and overall transparency to improve decision-making for the company as a whole and not only for one subsidiary,” said Lena Domhan, Global Process Owner Integrated Planning at STIHL. “Previously, we spent a lot of time analyzing and verifying data and had no single source of truth. In addition, disconnected planning systems hindered collaboration and coordination between subsidiaries.”

Market Trends Driving Change 
With the introduction of new product lines that have integrated batteries STIHL found itself in a new business, selling now also to end customers. “This VUCA world required us to react even faster to volatility and changing demand,” explained Axel Erhard, Senior Vice President, Global ONE STIHL Program Lead. “To optimize planning, we needed a better overview of the different production and sales companies.”

The company established the ONE STIHL program with the goal to consolidate all subsystems across 35 sales and 8 production companies into one single integrated SAP S/4HANA system. Moreover, STIHL aims to bring several solutions into one hybrid ecosystem, such as SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) with the components S&OP (sales & operations planning) and R&S (response & supply), SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling and SAP S/4 HANA Transportation Management (SAP TM). “One goal of the ONE STIHL project is to reduce fixed capital and get more transparency on different management levels. SAP IBP with SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling play a crucial role in this process, serving as the starting point for planning," said Axel.

Introducing a Synchronized Planning Approach for Distribution Center Refillment
“We were one of the first customers to introduce SAP IBP in combination with SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling and SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management,” explained Fabio Fender, Interim Global Process Owner Integrated Planning. “With real-time integration, our planners can quickly react, e.g., when tackling issues on the planning or production side they immediately see the impact on the distribution side and vice-versa. That provides confidence and trust.” Georg Fuchs, Director Integrated Planning & Global Solution Architect, ONE STIHL Project, added “order-based planning and real-time integration are the heartbeat of our production. For instance, in Austria finished goods are produced and then shipped to distribution centers in Germany or the Czech Republic. The order-based planning process in SAP IBP is used to allocate, prioritize, and schedule the delivery of these goods. We are de-coupling this from our production plan in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling.”

With this synchronized planning approach STIHL consolidates supply planning, distribution planning and production planning into one seamless process, enabling a holistic workspace for planners. Detailed scheduling results from SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling are visible in SAP IBP in real-time, ensuring maximum transparency. In addition, the integration of SAP TM allows to create freight orders to ship STIHL’s finished products. Axel commented “STIHL’s vertically integrated supply chain gives us the potential for end-to-end control.”

Higher Transparency across Subsidiaries enabling accurate Planning
SAP IBP is live at two production sites in Austria and Switzerland and used by several planners and other users utilizing its reporting capabilities. “The integration with SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling and SAP S/4HANA TM was the most effective solution to meet our business needs,” remarked Georg.

STIHL already recognizes and anticipates a range of immediate and long-term benefits:

  • Better visibility across subsidiaries, enabling accurate and feasible plans that can adapt to production constraints
  • Increased efficiency as sales and production teams use the same system with one single source of truth for all relevant data
  • End-to end transparency for global planning processes with optimized planning for the network 
  • Time-savings for planners who see real-time impact of changes and can instantly make better-informed decisions
  • Enables creation of feasible production plans to meet customer demand
  • Real-time integration with SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling ensures that planners and production teams are using the same data for decision-making
  • Logistics planners can efficiently plan truck load orders and promptly fulfill logistics requirements.
  • Improved customer service while reducing inventory costs through network planning
  • Enhanced decision-making between quantity and revenue planning providing a crucial element of these business processes and reporting.

Future Rollout Plans and Leveraging Artificial Intelligence
“We have 4-5 rollouts ahead of us on our journey. Over the next few years, we will integrate about 40 global subsidiaries into one network planning solution,” said Lena.

In relation to the utilization of artificial intelligence, Axel remarked “STIHL will invest in AI in the future, that is a clear focus of our owners.“ “We are starting to explore potential opportunities to determine what would benefit us. However, regarding SAP IBP, we are already fit for the future, concluded Fabio.”

 

Click here to see what benefits other companies have gained with SAP Integrated Business Planning. Also, read a report from IDC on the role of AI in supply chain planning by clicking here.

Watch a video to see the future of supply chain planning, powered by SAP Integrated Business Planning and infused with the latest SAP Business AI technology, to drive decision making in your supply chain.

In addition, check out the story by clicking here.

entry.title: Re: Fixed bin replenishment - How to prevent using source...
entry.content:
Hi.

You need to create a search sequence by storage type according to the replenishment movement, for example:

Replenishment is executed with movement type 320, then you need to set up a new search sequence for that movement:

In this image we see that when we make a Stock Removal movement, the system searches in storage types 311 (fixed bins) and 315, but when replenishment movement 320 is executed with Stock Removal movement, the system only searches in warehouse type 312.

And the E process - Putaway, system looks for a bin in the storage type 311 (fixed bins)

entry.title: IPS transformation assignGroup and multiple groups assignment
entry.content:
Hi All

We are trying to assign groups to users via transformation. It works if no conditions are used. But  if we have more than one condition, only one group is assigned. SCIM 2.0 is used.

We have following gorups

on IAS

On Onprem 

Z_ABAP_GROUP_DEV,

Z_ABAP_GROUP_TST

User has both groups assigned on ABAP system. 

On source system transformation we have 

 

 

{
"targetPath": "$.groups[0].value",
"condition": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[?(@.AGR_NAME == 'Z_ABAP_GROUP_TST)] EMPTY false",
"constant": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84",
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.groups[1].value",
"condition": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[?(@.AGR_NAME == 'Z_ABAP_GROUP_DEV')] EMPTY false",
"constant": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374",
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
}

 

 

And this works.

Payload is send to the Target transformation, both groups included

 

on Target side we have 

 

 

{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
},
{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
}

 

 

In result only one group (with id xxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374 - second form transformation - TST)  is assigned to the user 

If user has only one OnPrem group assigned (...DEV with id xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84 first in transformation) this group is assigned properly. 

 

 

Any idea how to fix this ? (Ticket to SAP raised) 

Following code if first "targetVariable": "assignGroup" is removed doesn't works as well 

 

 

{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxxx-xxxxxx-xxx-b94c-62bb6623cc84"
}
]
},
{
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374')] EMPTY false",
"constant": [
{
"id": "xxxxxxx-xxxxx-xxxx-9074-ed731f26b374"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"
}

 

 

Regards

Artur

entry.title: SAP Cloud SDK: How to setup requests using the business user instead of communication user (CAP)
entry.content:
Hi community,

I am using SAP Cloud SDK (with Node.js) and want to make a request to S4H Public Cloud with the business user instead of a communication user.

The setup looks like follows:

  • The project is a MTA with a Node.js server and a SAPUI5 application
  • The SAPUI5 application uses a destination with authentification type SAMLAssertion to make calls to OData services which are available on S4H Public Cloud
    • The logged on business user of the S4H Public Cloud system is getting used
    • The destination was setup like described here: Create a Destination to Connect to SAP Business Application Studio | SAP Help Portal
    • This works without problems
  • The SAPUI5 apps then also calls the Node.js server for certain things
    • The Node.js server is using the SAP Cloud SDK to make calls to released OData API services of S4H Public Cloud
    • For this a destination with a communication user is used right now
      • Needed communication arrangement with that comm.user are maintained on the S4H Public Cloud system
      • This works wihtout problems
      • Now I would like to make certain calls to those APIs with the business user (which is logged on in the SAPUI5 app) instead of the communication user
        • I made sure that the APIs support that (a business catalog with the the authorization to call those APIs exists - so the business user is actually allowed to call those APIs if he has assigned a corresponding role which includes that business catalog)
        • I was able to use the SAMLAssertion destination in the SAPUI5 application to call the APIs directly in there - it worked
          • But I would now also like to be able to do the same thing on the Node.js server
          • I'm struggling how to setup the connections (forwarding tokens?)

The part for the connection between the SAPUI5 app and the Node.js server inside xs-app.json looks like follows:

 {
      "source": "^/admin/(.*)$",
      "target": "/admin/$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": true
    }
 
The SAMLAssertion destination on the BTP subaccount looks like follows:

 

 
Has anyone tried this before? Any help is very appreciated!
 
Thank you
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Employee unable to login with SSO in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
As mentioned above , there could be many reasons for the error so its impossible to give an accurate answer here. Also, since it is an open community, its best not to share details specific to the user here - which would allow you to troubleshoot it. 

In addition to checking out the KBA that is referenced above I would suggest to 

1- Start with running a SAML trace for the affected user to see what is actually happening with the user. This can help to figure out where is the user being sent to authenticate and possibly you can spot the reason here. If not, you can always export the results to diagnose with your internal IT team or SAP Support 

2- Check the users account status - the error message below states that user account might not be active. If you are using Identity Authentication to authenticate then within that application you will be able to check whether the user having issues have their credentials stored in the user store for that application. if it is not included there then this could be the reason. 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Wrong format in excel download
entry.content:
Hello,

OLE technology is not available within browsers, consequently not available within webgui/Fiori

  • OLE Automation Controller

The controlling of an OLE automation server is not possible.

  • Office Integration

Because Office Integration is based on OLE Automation (a legacy Microsoft Windows technology not available within browsers) it is limited within SAP GUI for HTML. Office Documents can only be viewed but not be changed. 

 

The only workaround available:

3314872 - SAP Desktop Office Integration Support for SAP GUI for HTML and SAP GUI for Java

-> https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-enable-the-sap-desktop-office-integration-for-sap-gui-for-html-and/ba-p/13566119

entry.title: Where can I start learning principles and strategies of SAP Ariba Category Management?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing SAP Ariba Category Management Fundamentals learning journey.

Overview
This course will teach the learner how to create, manage, and monitor category-specific strategies to achieve their organization’s procurement goals.

Learning objectives
Upon the completion of this learning journey, participants will gain insight into how a Category Manager creates a category profile by classifying a product category, uploading company policies, laws, and regulations concerning that specific category, and adding team members and stakeholders that will assist with the execution of the strategy. Then, develop a strategy and plan, including category and market analysis, strategy and implementation, and approval. Finally, monitor the strategy execution to see if goals are met, initiatives are on target, and upcoming events are posted in the planning calendar. 

Prerequisites

  • Learning Journey: Discovering Category Management

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

I just tested this in a cloud system and do not get the error that you mentioned. You may want to open a ticket with PM-WOC-MN to get support from the application colleagues that provide the view.

Generally, I would not recommend adding fields by following such deep paths. Apart from this, you will multiply your result set as you will get the text in ALL languages unless you filter by the language afterwards. Try adding the text view by defining your own 1..1 association directly to it instead. This may also resolve this inconsistency error.

Best Regards
Andreas

entry.title: Extending the reach of enterprise systems in communication challenged environments
entry.content:
For over two decades SAP has been providing solutions to customers in defence and other industries the ability to operate in communication challenged environments. SAP has worked directly with customers as well as through the Defence Interest Group which represents our military customers and provide consolidated requirements on this topic. While advances have been made in establishing pervasive networks, be it garrison or at remote sites, there are still several situations where connectivity to enterprise systems prevents direct access to them:

  • the type of connectivity often varies by location or situation
  • data transmission costs may be too high for limited budgets
  • data transmission is degraded or not permitted for safety or operational reasons
  • transmission protocols restrict how data can be transmitted and stored
  • backend systems are not available (planned or unplanned)

These situations can occur within the national boundaries of a customer or when they operate elsewhere around the globe.

In contrast to individuals, teams have the added requirement to share data and information even in communication challenged environments. This is because enterprise processes are typically conducted by more than one individual and as such, there is a need for shared situational awareness. For example, when fulfilling demand, one must wait till the goods receipt has been recorded to record a goods issue, especially when it’s important to track against batch or serial numbers.

As such, people need to continue to work in these communications challenged environments:

  • individuals for a few minutes or hours while they are away from their “desk” working in zones without network connectivity. These could be on base or at remote site on operation or exercise where there is little to no network or it’s unsafe to transmit due to the proximity to explosives. Another example is an air force crew bringing an offline device to keep track of maintenance activities and parts consumed while away on missions. Typically, these individuals would synchronize after their return to their “desk”. SAP customers, across a multitude of industries, rely on SAP applications to enable their remote field workers to maintain their own and their customer assets.
  • Teams for a few days to one or several weeks away from their home base. This could be a team deployed on exercise or operation such as in humanitarian assistance, peacekeeping, or higher intensity operation. Be it a land, air, naval or joint deployment. Small teams such as on a small vessel or an air task group or much larger deployments. These include naval customers who manage the logistics process on board their vessels on deployments lasting multiple months. Similarly, teams in the commercial shipping and oil & gas industries use SAP solution to manage their logistics processes offline.

 

Typically, synchronization occurs on a periodic basis (e.g. daily or less frequently) or on an ad-hoc basis whenever individuals or teams can establish a connection to synchronize. In rare cases, they would synchronize only upon their return to their home base. There are no technical limits to how long individuals and teams can operate without synchronizations. This is because at some point there is a need for these individuals or teams to request supplies or maintenance as they are no longer fully self-sufficient.

Broadly, customers look to SAP for the following to enable logistics processes:

  • Provide individuals and teams the ability to have access to business essential functions (i.e. a subset) of their enterprise system while operating in communication challenged environments
  • Provide end-users a resilient, modern, and consistent user experience across devices
  • Provide a resilient, modern, and consistent technology platform that customers can extend and integrate with other systems (e.g. IoT and Battle Management) as well as manage and support them in a distributed IT landscape

SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security and SAP Service & Asset Manager are the key enablers for teams and individuals respectively.

These products provide technology to establish and synchronize data across the communication challenged landscape:

  • Identify the sufficient master and transactional data subset from the enterprise system to be transferred to the system of the individual and the team to minimize the IT footprint
  • Detect and synchronize new and changed data to the relevant recipients while maintaining transactional integrity
  • Establish mechanisms to minimize the occurrences of data synchronization errors from data conflicts

Our customers can operate in a variety of communication challenged environments and provide the tools for users to seamlessly work regardless of the status of the network. The IT organization has in their hands familiar technologies to manage their SAP landscape. Finally, Commanders at all levels can make better readiness based decisions from data from the edge.

entry.title: Experience the Benefits of SAP Software with our new Interactive Value Journeys
entry.content:
Introduction

 

The International Business Unit Defense and Security (D&S) is delighted to announce that the new Interactive Value Journeys (IVJs) for the Defense and Security industry are now publicly accessible online. These IVJs provide comprehensive insights and tailored solutions, empowering organizations within the industry to leverage best practices and innovative strategies for overcoming their organizational challenges and problems. By making these resources available, SAP aims to support the continuous improvement and operational excellence of our clients, thereby contributing to the overall security and stability of global Defense and Security industry.

 

 

What are Interactive Value Journeys and for what can they be used?

 

Figure 1: Interacive Value Journey for Operational Readiness

 

Interactive Value Journeys (IVJs) are advanced, interactive customer journeys or demos that allow us to showcase the following to customers, prospective clients, partners, and other interested parties:

  • Core Products and Solutions: An overview of the core products and solutions SAP currently offers for Defense and Security customers, including armed forces organizations, emergency response teams, border control, and customs services.

  • Key Advantages and Differentiation: Highlighting the main advantages of SAP’s offerings and how they differ from competitors' solutions.

  • Realistic Implementation Scenarios: Demonstrating typical implementation scenarios, providing a realistic view of how these solutions can be deployed and utilized.

  • Recent Innovations: Showcasing SAP's latest innovations and advancements tailored for the D&S industry.

 

 

The new Defense and Security Interactive Value Journeys

 

Figure 2: Example Interactive Value Journey

 

In total, we offer 9 distinct IVJs covering the following topic areas:

1. More Effective Force Generation and Deployment with SAP: Link

 

Figure 3: More Effective Force Generation and Deployment with SAP IVJ

 

This IVJ demonstrates how SAP S/4HANA Defense and Security enables our clients to digitize and enhance the force generation and force deployment process holistically with our ERP software solution. By implementing our Enterprise Resource Planning software, our clients can benefit from structured real-time management of their forces, vehicles (reaching from cars and trucks, over ships to airplanes), and other assets during day-to-day operations, trainings, exercises, or active missions. SAP S/4HANA also ensures maximum interoperability within and beyond the organization, facilitating seamless coordination with alliances and partners.

 

2. Ensure Full Operational Readiness of your D&S Organization: Link 

 

Figure 4: Ensure Full Operational Readiness of your D&S Organization IVJ

 

Operational readiness is highly critical for any Defense and Security (D&S) organization, whether military or emergency services. With its decades-long experience in this and other industries, SAP enables the monitoring, administration, and optimization of operational readiness for each unit, as well as the readiness of vehicles, equipment, and other assets, using a centralized, intuitive software application. This IVJ showcases a typical scenario in this area, highlighting the benefits of such solutions and demonstrating how they can be effectively implemented by clients.

 

3. Strengthen and Secure your Critical Supply Chains with SAP: Link

 

Figure 5: Strengthen and Secure your Critical Supply Chains with SAP IVJ

 

SAP is a leading software vendor in the areas of Procurement, Transportation, Warehousing, and Supply Chain Management. It offers Defense and Security organizations access to best-in-class software solutions, thereby enabling for an end-to-end optimization of their logistics processes. Our clients benefit from a wide range of innovations, such as Artificial Intelligence functions and features, automated document processing, and the direct integration of Internet of Things (IoT), Digital Twin technology, or Robotic Process Automation (RPA) devices into the system. This IVJ demonstrates a typical scenario of implementing a logistics software solution in a D&S organization.

 

4. Create Seamless Interoperability Across Organizations: Link 

 

Figure 6: Create Seamless Interoperability Across Organizations IVJ

 

SAP Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) solutions for Defense and Security (D&S) organizations are built on the foundation of maximum interoperability. From an IT perspective, this includes the ability to deploy the software as a cloud or on-premises solution and to develop customized applications for specific processes and tasks. Moreover, it enables the direct integration of all core stakeholders into the system by using existing functions and interfacing, or by integrating specific APIs. This integration extends beyond IT functionality to encompass operational partners such as suppliers in the procurement area, as well as alliances and partners. The interoperable architecture of SAP S/4HANA Defense and Security facilitates real-time data exchange between two or more D&S organizations, allowing them to jointly conduct operations, trainings, exercises, and active missions. This functionality is available even in communication-challenged environments through the SAP Disconnected Operations solutions. Our IVJ for interoperability highlights the various advantages of such solutions for D&S clients.

 

5. SAP Business AI for Defense and Security Organizations: Link 

 

Figure 7: SAP Business AI for Defense and Security Organizations IVJ

 

Artificial Intelligence has been a global megatrend since the emergence of OpenAI's Generative AI-powered ChatGPT application in late 2022. What many people might not know is that SAP has been a leading player in the field of Artificial Intelligence, offering numerous Machine Learning and traditional AI solutions to its clients for a long time. To further enhance its applications and services for clients, SAP has decided to integrate Artificial Intelligence, and here in particular Generative Artificial Intelligence, into all of its core solutions and products. Examples of this include Embedded AI functions within applications like S/4HANA, the SAP Joule Copilot, which allows users to interact with applications in natural language, as well as many more innovations. The above Interactive Value Journey for SAP Business AI highlights the AI products and services offered to Defense and Security clients, potential implementation scenarios, and the advantages our customers can gain with the technology.

 

6. SAP Sustainability Solutions for D&S Organizations: Link

 

Figure 8: SAP Sustainability Solutions for D&S Organizations IVJ

 

Sustainability is a top priority for the majority of governments, companies, and organizations globally. SAP is proud that its SAP Sustainability Solutions are positioned in the Leaders category for worldwide carbon accounting and management software applications in the 2024 IDC MarketScape Vendor Assessment. The above IVJ provides insights into how D&S organizations can use our sustainability software to monitor, analyze, and manage all aspects of sustainability in the most efficient and transparent way possible. This enables our customers to meet international, national, and internal environmental, sustainability, and climate goals, thereby enhancing operational readiness, mission effectiveness, and contributing to global security and stability.

 

7. SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing Solutions: Link 

 

Figure 9: SAP Employee Health and Wellbeing Solutions IVJ

 

With this Interactive Value Journey, we aim to inform interested parties about the possibilities and benefits of SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing solutions. This comprehensive software package enables organizations to enhance the wellbeing, safety, and health of their entire workforce, regardless of their size. The IVJ illustrates how D&S organizations can use these solutions to address challenges such as high rates of sick leave, employee turnover, and general workplace safety issues, while simultaneously making the employer more attractive to potential applicants. SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing solutions streamline the management of organizational wellbeing by integrating distributed systems and data into a unified application. This integration facilitates the monitoring and improvement of each employee's wellbeing within the organization. Additionally, it helps identify critical pain points, such as employee safety concerns, ultimately enhancing employee retention by creating better working conditions.

8. Improve your Auditing and Reporting Processes with SAP: Link 

 

Figure 10: Improve your Auditing and Reporting Processes with SAP

 

The above IVJ showcases how the SAP Auditing and Reporting solutions, as an important part of the SAP Financial Management portfolio, can empower Defense and Security (D&S) organizations to achieve greater transparency and operational efficiency. These solutions enable D&S organizations to adhere to various external and internal compliance standards while enhancing their risk management from day-to-day operations to specific international missions. For example, they facilitate the constant monitoring of critical projects within the organization and ensure adherence to internal rules and guidelines, such as budget restrictions. Overall, this can help D&S organizations reinforce trust and accountability within their operations.

 

9. Detecting and Mitigating Natural Catastrophes with EDiSON: Link

 

Figure 11: Detecting and Mitigating Natural Catastrophes with EDiSON

 

Disaster prevention and effective disaster response, such as responding to natural catastrophes, are typically among the core tasks of Defense and Security organizations. For this reason, SAP has teamed up with Japan's Oita University to offer its clients the innovative, AI-powered Earth Disaster Intelligent System Operational Network (EDiSON). This software application measurably enhances the detection and mitigation of natural disasters. As demonstrated in the IVJ, EDiSON includes various innovative and helpful functions and features, such as a comprehensive dashboard, the ability to create forecasts for the course of extreme weather events, and different forms of impact analyses. The AI-driven EDiSON solution thereby relies on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for services like automation, analytics, Artificial Intelligence, data management, and application development.

 

 

Further Information

 

If you have any additional questions about SAP Defense and Security software solutions, please visit our website or reach out directly to us by clicking on the following link, or by adding a comment to this blog post.

Additional information about the SAP Business Applications, and the most recent offerings and innovations from SAP can be found below:

  • SAP Enterprise Resource Planning Website 
  • RISE with SAP Website 
  • GROW with SAP Website 
  • SAP Business AI Website 
  • SAP Unstoppable Website 
  • SAP Sustainability Solutions Website 
  • SAP Industry Solutions Website 
  • SAP Partner Portal

entry.title: Design Reports - How many days ago the last sales order was created
entry.content:
Hello Design Reports Experts!!

I would like to create an index (Key Figure) to display how many days ago the last sales order was created for the customer via Key Figure in a report.
The idea would be to first perform a filter to list only the last sales order created for the customer, and then use the “created on” field of the sales order to perform the calculation.

Example image:
Customer placed the last order on 12/3/2024.
Today's date: 12/5/2024
Expected result in a given Key Figure: 2 days
Idea of ​​calculation to be performed: 12/5/2024 - 12/3/2024 = difference of 2 days.

Thanks a lot for the help.

Clóvis Müller

entry.title: New podcast - SAP Learning Ins Artificial Intelligence Adoption with Thomas Jenewein
entry.content:
Dear Learners,

I am glad to inform you that we have recently been published on SAP Podcasts

SAP Learning Insights Artificial Intelligence Adoption with Thomas Jenewein

In this episode of SAP Learning Insights, David Chaviano welcomes Thomas Jenewein to discuss the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) within organizations. They explore the psychological aspects of AI implementation, emphasizing the importance of understanding diverse stakeholder perspectives for successful integration.
Thomas shares real-world examples demonstrating how AI can enhance productivity and creativity while addressing employee concerns about job security. The conversation also highlights the role of digital natives in driving AI change and innovative training methods for effective learning. David and Thomas advocate for a human-centered approach to AI, encouraging organizations to leverage it as a partner in innovation.

Tune in for key insights on navigating AI implementation in the workplace!

How to get started SAP Learning here are some resources:

entry.title: The importance of CNAME, Certificates and Custom API Domain Prefix
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! 

Securing customer data while providing a smooth and reliable user experience is essential for every business. SAP Customer Data Cloud offers solutions that not only protect sensitive information but also ensure brand consistency and user trust through configurations like CNAME, SSL certificates, and custom API domain prefixes.

A CNAME (Canonical Name) acts as an alias, connecting your domain’s subdomain to another domain. Within SAP Customer Data Cloud, this setup eliminates browser warnings about third-party cookies, an issue frequently encountered in privacy-focused browsers like Safari or during incognito browsing. By using a CNAME, API calls and social logins appear to come directly from your domain, keeping interactions branded and professional.

Adding SSL certificates to SAP Customer Data Cloud strengthens data encryption, protecting exchanges between users, systems, and the platform. For example, creating a CNAME like identity.mysite.com ensures that all interactions route securely through your domain, avoiding cookie-blocking issues and enhancing the user experience.

The platform also enables configurations like trusted sites and custom API domain prefixes for added security and control. Trusted sites ensure all domains involved in your data exchanges are verified, while a custom API domain prefix—like identity.mysite.com—ensures your branding is reflected in every interaction. These configurations are ideal for features like social logins, hosted pages, centralized login, and Single Sign-On (SSO).

With SAP Customer Data Cloud, these configurations go beyond enhancing security. They enable seamless integrations with external platforms like Facebook and Apple, comply with evolving browser standards, and improve customer journeys across mobile and desktop devices. Implementing these best practices ensures a secure, branded, and user-friendly experience.

To discover how SAP Customer Data Cloud can transform your user experience and help configure these features, visit The importance of CNAME, Certificates and Custom API Domain Prefix on the SAP Learning Hub.

Access and Support

To access the link above, complete a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub for SAP Enterprise Support. A step-by-step guide is available here.

Need Help?

Have questions? Reach out to the SAP Value Map team or sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps to explore additional resources.

entry.title: Re: CCM - Settlement document with negative and positive...
entry.content: hello yes i did!  there is two alternatives  alternative 1 - i didnt test this one Settlement Management   -> Condition Contract Management ->  Condition Contract Settlement -> Define and Configure Profiles for Business Volume Determination Move the value into an existing header field which you don’t use, e.g. WBRK-KIDNO. Alternatively create a new Z-field. Use the SHKZG to head.  And i did not know this at the time and the alternative that i did was ABAP.  I did it via enhancement WB2_CC_SETTL_CHANGE_FINALLY, I did the code below, if you need it. Code used:     READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke INDEX 1.     IF ls_komzrke-lfart = '0S12'.       LOOP AT ct_komzrpe INTO ls_komzrpe.         lv_oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.         IF ls_komzrpe-itemcat = 1.           ls_komzrpe-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr + 1.           MODIFY ct_komzrpe FROM ls_komzrpe INDEX sy-tabix.           LOOP AT ct_komlfkoec INTO ls_komlfkoec                                WHERE oldpo = ls_komzrpe-oldpo.             ls_komlfkoec-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.             MODIFY ct_komlfkoec FROM ls_komlfkoec INDEX sy-tabix.           ENDLOOP.           READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke                                 WITH KEY oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.           IF sy-subrc <> 0.             READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke                         WITH KEY oldnr = lv_oldnr.             ls_komzrke-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.             APPEND ls_komzrke TO ct_komzrke.           ENDIF.         ENDIF.       ENDLOOP.     ENDIF.

entry.title: Master Data Management: The Foundation for Modern Enterprise Data Architecture
entry.content:
Within an enterprise, critical data such as customer information, product details, asset records, and location data is used across various business units and operational systems. Managing this "master data" to ensure its accuracy, timeliness, and consistency is the core function of Master Data Management (MDM). This process, complemented by robust data governance practices, ensures that the responsibility for data quality is appropriately managed and verified. Over the past two decades, the software industry has provided systems to support MDM, but what functionalities are essential for a modern MDM system today?

Core Functions of a Modern MDM System

Data Integration and Cleansing

A modern MDM system should access data from operational systems and bring it together in a master data hub. This involves loading and synchronizing data, cleansing it to correct errors, and enriching records—for instance, filling missing postal codes, tax IDs or adding demographic information – leveraging data quality services, like SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data, or third party data providers, like CDQ (Corporate Data Quality).

Data Matching and Merging

With master data distributed across numerous systems, identifying duplicate records is crucial. The system should be capable of merging candidate records into a single, trusted master record (sometimes called the “best record” or “golden record”) through predefined rules based on criteria like how recently they were updated or their completeness and, sometimes, human intervention.

Data Governance and Workflow

A modern MDM system should support data governance processes, including access controls, data ownership, and audit trails. Automated, customizable workflow features for data stewards to manage data issues and approvals for changes are also essential. This allows various teams to own unique master data attributes and enforce validated values for specific data points through collaborative workflow routing and notification.

Hierarchy and Classification Management

Managing hierarchies of classification (e.g., legal business hierarchies, retail shop locations, customer classifications) is a key feature. The system should handle complex, unbalanced hierarchies efficiently, including the ability to retrieve legal hierarchies from external data providers like a Dun & Bradstreet so that the MDM system can load that data, solve any conflicts, and replicate the hierarchy information to consuming applications.

Analytics and Reporting

The system should provide intuitive analytics and reporting capabilities, highlighting data quality metrics and trends over time.  The MDM solution should allow data experts to define, validate, and monitor established business rules to confirm master data readiness and quality.  There should also be the ability analyze the performance of master data management processes (i.e. number of open master data change requests or the average time to close a change request).

Scalability and Deployment Options

A modern MDM system should be scalable and support both on-premise and cloud-based deployment options, adapting as source data migrates between different platforms.

Security and Usability

The system should have role-based security, ensuring only authorized users can perform specific tasks. An intuitive, customizable user interface and industry or data domain-specific templates can speed up deployment.

Supporting Current Initiatives

A robust MDM system supports digitalization, cloud migration, and business process automation. It also provides high-quality data for training AI models, making the AI more effective. As businesses increasingly adopt generative AI, having a reliable MDM system ensures that the AI models are trained on accurate, trustworthy data – and the most important data is your business-critical master data.

MDM and Data Fabric

The data fabric approach aims to provide business users direct access to corporate data assets without traditional data warehouses. However, the semantic layer of a data fabric must navigate numerous underlying operational systems to determine the most up-to-date and trustworthy data. An MDM system is instrumental here, as it carries out the critical work of cleaning, merging, and enriching data to create "golden records." By using master data from the MDM hub, the data fabric's semantic layer can navigate enterprise data assets more effectively, simplifying queries and providing accurate, consistent data for business users.

MDM as a Foundation for Generative AI

Generative AI, based on large language models (LLMs), has the potential to revolutionize various industries. However, LLMs frequently produce unreliable content due to poor-quality training data from publicly available sources. Corporations are now training LLMs on corporate-specific data for better accuracy.

MDM systems provide the high-quality, timely, and accurate data needed to train these LLMs effectively. Companies with well-managed MDM hubs have a competitive advantage, as their AI models perform significantly better with reliable data foundations.

Conclusion

Master data management remains a cornerstone of modern enterprise data architecture. Whether implementing a data fabric or training generative AI, having a well-managed master data hub is essential. The ability to access, clean, and enrich data ensures that enterprises can leverage advanced technologies effectively, driving innovation and maintaining a competitive edge. Investing in MDM is not just a safeguard for data quality; it is a strategic move that underpins numerous modern data initiatives.

Read the full paper on the capabilities and benefits of a modern master data management system here and listen to our podcast.

Learn more about SAP’s solution for master data management, SAP Master Data Governance, at www.sap.com/mdg.
